description_long: This is a vintage-style gramophone record player featuring a large, flared yellow horn made of metal or painted brass, which serves as the sound amplifier. The base is a rectangular, black wooden box with decorative turned wooden columns at each corner and a small metallic plaque on the front. On top of the base sits a circular black turntable platter with a visible vinyl record, and a yellow tonearm extending from the side, positioned over the record. A small crank handle protrudes from one side, suggesting manual winding operation. The overall design is ornate and classic, evoking early 20th-century aesthetics, and the object appears sturdy and well-crafted.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large yellow horn, black wooden base, and visible vinyl record. Features ornate columns, a crank handle, and a yellow tonearm.
description_view_0: Front view showing the yellow horn, black platter with record, yellow tonearm, and metallic plaque on the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the horn's curvature, the crank handle, and the decorative columns on the base.
description_view_2: Rear view with the horn's back, the tonearm's attachment, and the plain back of the base.
description_view_3: Top-down view focusing on the wide opening of the yellow horn and the circular platter with record.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and black, occupying the central top area of the base. It appears to be made of plastic or metal, with a smooth, glossy finish and a visible vinyl record placed on top. The platter is proportionally smaller than the base but large enough to accommodate a standard record. No distinct grooves or patterns are visible on the platter itself, but the record has a central label.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, yellow in color, and extends from the right side of the base over the platter. It is curved, moderately thick, and appears to be made of metal or painted plastic. The tonearm is positioned above the record, suggesting it is ready to play, and has a visible joint where it attaches to the base.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is not clearly visible, but its presence is implied at the end of the tonearm where it meets the record.
